result:
I have everything needed. Here's the trace.

## x86_64 `read()` syscall path

**Entry point:** `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, unsigned int, fd, char __user *, buf, size_t, count)` — `fs/read_write.c:621`

This macro (`SYSCALL_DEFINE3` → `SYSCALL_DEFINEx` → `__SYSCALL_DEFINEx`, defined in `include/linux/syscalls.h:221-230`) expands to a function literally named `sys_read`. On x86_64, `arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h` further wraps it via `__SYS_STUBx`/`SYSCALL_DEFINEx` so the actual symbol placed in the dispatch table is `__x64_sys_read`, which just calls into the `sys_read` body.

**Call chain (named function hops, in order):**

1. `__x64_sys_read` / `sys_read` (body defined by `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)`) — `fs/read_write.c:621`
2. `ksys_read(fd, buf, count)` — called at `fs/read_write.c:623`; defined at `fs/read_write.c:602`
3. `vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos)` — called at `fs/read_write.c:613`; defined at `fs/read_write.c:450`

So: `sys_read` (621) → `ksys_read` (602) → `vfs_read` (450), all in `fs/read_write.c`. Confirmed via `codegraph callees ksys_read` (shows `vfs_read` at `fs/read_write.c:450`) and `codegraph callers vfs_read` (shows `ksys_read` at `fs/read_write.c:602` as one of the three callers, alongside `ksys_pread64` and `exec.c`'s `read_code`, which are separate paths not part of this trace).

## Syscall-number → handler mapping

The in-tree (non-generated) source of truth is:

**`arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.tbl:11`**
```
0	common	read			sys_read
```

This `.tbl` file assigns syscall number **0** to `read`, mapped to symbol `sys_read`. It is not itself generated — it's the hand-maintained table checked into the tree. A build-time script (`scripts/syscalltbl.sh`, invoked from the build) reads this `.tbl` file and generates `arch/x86/include/generated/asm/syscalls_64.h`, which `arch/x86/entry/syscall_64.c` includes twice (once via `__SYSCALL(nr, sym) extern long __x64_##sym(...)` to declare, once via `__SYSCALL(nr, sym) __x64_##sym,` to populate `sys_call_table[]`). That table entry at index 0 resolves to `__x64_sys_read`, closing the loop back to the `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)` site.
